repo_prior_art.cite: pid_network_exposure/v1 Conjecture 1: shared-edge compatibility makes cellwise bounds nonsharp

published_prior_art.cite: SchroderOprescuFeuerriegelKallus2026 gives sharp bounds for fixed canonical exposure-mapping misspecification, not a shared sparse K-support latent-map versus cellwise frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_mediation_proxy/v1 Theorem 1 has nonrectangular direct/indirect geometry; pid_network_exposure/v1 has shared network-completion nonsharpness, but not this joint latent-exposure DI vector

published_prior_art.cite: HuLiWager2022 defines ADE/AIE and a Bernoulli policy-effect decomposition under specified exposure structure, not joint nonrectangular partial-ID under latent labels
BeresteanuMolchanovMolinari2011 plus Edmonds1965/Wolsey1975 supply the random-set and discrete-certificate ingredients, but not the combined causal sparse K-label endpoint certificate

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposal cites Edmonds1965 as the matching-polytope certificate source, but the listed paper is Paths, Trees, and Flowers; the matching-polytope facet reference should be Edmonds's separate 1965 maximum-matching polyhedron paper or the claim should be narrowed to blossom/matching feasibility.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 3
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship certificate claim cites Edmonds1965 and Wolsey1975 without section, theorem, equation, or page anchors tying those papers to the exact b-matching / lifted-cover certificate used.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 3
soundness_flags.one_line: As stated, every endpoint failure t > h_K(u;P) must have a sparse-network incompatibility certificate, but failures can arise solely from bounded-response or observed-law constraints; the claim needs to restrict to endpoints feasible for the cellwise/random-set relaxation but infeasible for shared sparse K-label compatibility.

soundness_flags.label: Corollary no-indirect
soundness_flags.one_line: The scalar Delta_K(P) is defined as a max over all audit directions, so 'Delta_K(P)=0 for all directions involving only indirect contrasts' is not a well-typed reduction; replace it with the directional gap h_rect,K(u;P)-h_K(u;P)=0 for indirect-only u.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair Conjecture 3 by defining a cellwise-feasible endpoint class E_cell(u,P) and stating that endpoints in E_cell(u,P) above h_K have either an Aumann separation or a sparse K-label incidence certificate, with the network-only certificate reserved for endpoints whose outcome/random-set inequalities are otherwise feasible.
Add exact location anchors for the certificate comparators: the matching/blossom or matching-polytope theorem used from Edmonds and the lifted-cover/facet result used from Wolsey.

Sharpen the repo-axis distinction from pid_network_exposure/v1 by making the K-support label system and joint DI support direction essential in the headline, not just an extension of shared-edge cellwise nonsharpness.

recommended_next_step: Patch Conjecture 3's quantifier/domain and certificate citations, then restate the no-indirect sanity check as a directional support-gap collapse.
